sample_posts.text: consumers are seemingly becoming more token price sensitive  https://x.com/trevornoren/status/2073031380187951502

tweet.text:
Markets were jittery this week on news reports that Meta may be selling compute, raising concerns about excess supply. We thought we’d share some perspective using our rental term curves on why this news, if true, doesn’t have to be bearish for GPU rental prices.

Last year, ahead of the agentic AI boom, Meta aggressively locked in a large amount of compute capacity. It quite possibly secured more than it ultimately needed. It was a smart strategic bet to secure scarce supply early, with the flexibility to either deploy it internally or sell excess into a tight market. They acquired valuable r

convergence.context: 4 posts across 3 channels (shoalresearch, leakmealpha, cookiesreads) — mentioning Haseeb Qureshi, 3meme, Pumponomics, PoorGoat

sample_posts.text:
Haseeb On Tokens vs Equity, Venice, and VVV
> “A lot of people’s discomfort with VVV is that they think every token should be a governance token or should basically be equity. If you think this, then you basically object to actual capital innovation in tokens”
> “Venice is not a DAO, its not a decentralised product… Its a fast growing AI startup that is using tokens as part of its capital stack”
Source:
https://x.com/hosseeb/status/2073447818564641211
